title: Cerulean Health — Data Retention, KMS & 'No-Train' Assurance
preview: Cerulean is validating Redwood's data handling controls: no-training guarantees, prompt retention and deletion, customer-managed keys (BYOK/HSM), audit log retention and tamper-evidence, SSO/RBAC mapping, and data residency options. Agreed to exchange SOC2, KMS integration notes, and run a short pilot with synthetic...
text: Cerulean is validating Redwood's data handling controls: no-training guarantees, prompt retention and deletion, customer-managed keys (BYOK/HSM), audit log retention and tamper-evidence, SSO/RBAC mapping, and data residency options. Agreed to exchange SOC2, KMS integration notes, and run a short pilot with synthetic PHI to validate operational behavior.

text:
Meeting header:
Date: 2025-09-09
Start time (UTC): 15:00
Duration: ~54 minutes
Attendees: Samira Patel (Redwood AE), Marcus Lin (Redwood SE), Anika Rao (Cerulean Head of Security), Jon Morales (Cerulean Platform Ops), Priya Desai (Cerulean Compliance)
Shared links: https://redwood.ai/docs/admin-export-playbook (deck), s3://redwood-samples/cerulean-export-sample.ndjson (sample)

Auto-summary (auto-generated, may be rough):
- Focus: what admin/admin-event logs are captured, export formats (ndjson/parquet), streaming vs batch exports, retention and archival options, encryption and KMS patterns, and proof/evidence for SOC2 controls.
- Actionable: Redwood to share schemas and run a pilot export to Cerulean's S3 bucket; Cerulean to confirm region and legal retention limits.

Topics: capture types; export destinations; retention & archival; encryption/KMS; RBAC/SSO linkage to events; evidenc...
